summary: model.py implements the Model layer (MVC) of a Calorie Calculator Python app. The code is functionally reasonable but has 3 HIGH-severity issues around data safety and performance, 5 MEDIUM issues around architecture and code style, and 4 LOW issues around readability.

issues.comment: In the load() method, catching KeyError at the top level means a single corrupted record in data.json causes the entire user dataset to be silently discarded and replaced with defaults.
issues.recommendation: Handle errors per-record inside the list comprehensions, skipping only malformed records and logging a warning. Separate KeyError from json.JSONDecodeError handling.

issues.comment: _load_defaults() calls self.save() unconditionally with no exception handling. If the filesystem is read-only or permissions are insufficient, the constructor raises an unhandled exception and the app crashes on first launch.
issues.recommendation: Wrap the self.save() call in _load_defaults() with try/except OSError and handle gracefully (e.g., log a warning).

issues.comment: recalc_water_from_meals calls get_food_by_name twice per meal entry inside a generator expression — once for the None guard and once for the getattr call. get_food_by_name performs a linear scan, making this O(n*m) with redundant work.
issues.recommendation: Store the result in a local variable inside a regular for-loop to avoid the double scan.

issues.comment: DATA_FILE = 'data.json' is relative to the current working directory. If the app is launched from a different directory, data will be silently lost or a new empty file created.
issues.recommendation: Use pathlib.Path(__file__).parent / 'data.json' to anchor the path relative to the module file.

issues.comment: Multiple CRUD methods use semicolons to combine two statements on one line (e.g. self.food_items.append(item); self.save()). This is against PEP 8 Compound Statements guidelines and reduces readability.
issues.recommendation: Split each statement onto its own line.

issues.comment: get_food_by_name performs a linear scan of food_items on every call. It is invoked inside analytics loops, creating O(n*m) complexity.
issues.recommendation: Maintain a dict[str, FoodItem] index keyed by name. Update it in add_food, edit_food, delete_food, and _load_defaults.

issues.comment: activity_heatmap, calories_vs_burned_by_date, and similar methods iterate the full log once per day, giving O(days * log_size) complexity. For a 12-week heatmap this is 84 full scans.
issues.recommendation: Pre-group entries by date using collections.defaultdict before iterating days.

issues.comment: The file does not end with a newline character (diff shows '\ No newline at end of file'). This violates the POSIX standard and PEP 8.
issues.recommendation: Add a trailing newline at the end of the file.

issues.comment: Comments and docstrings mix Ukrainian and English (e.g. sex: 'male' або 'female'). This inconsistency can confuse contributors.
issues.recommendation: Choose one language for all comments and docstrings and apply it consistently.

issues.comment: Many public methods use # inline comments instead of """docstrings""", making them invisible to help() and documentation generators.
issues.recommendation: Convert relevant # comments to proper docstrings on public methods.

issues.comment: In WeightEntry.__init__, self.note is assigned before self.date, which is inconsistent with the parameter order and to_dict output order.
issues.recommendation: Reorder attribute assignments to match the parameter declaration and to_dict output.

issues.comment: bmi_category and bmi_color use single-line 'if: return' statements, which PEP 8 discourages.
issues.recommendation: Use the standard multiline if/return format.
